reasons: Grock can create terrain pressure that helps Badang trap enemies near walls.
Badang can follow Grock's wall placement with crowd-control and burst pressure in a restricted area.

proof.summary: Grock creates terrain and limits escape paths, while Badang punishes enemies trapped near walls.
worksBestWhen: Grock places the wall to limit escape paths.
Badang is close enough to follow immediately.
failureCases: Grock's wall blocks his own team's follow-up.
Badang is not ready when the wall lands.
Enemies blink over or around the terrain.

reasons: Grock can wall off movement routes and make enemy pathing more predictable for Pharsa.
Pharsa can punish enemies that Grock restricts near choke points or objective entrances.

proof.summary: Grock limits enemy movement with terrain, while Pharsa punishes the restricted area from long range.
worksBestWhen: Grock walls narrow entrances near objectives.
Pharsa positions safely before Grock commits.
failureCases: Grock walls at the wrong angle.
Enemies disengage before follow-up damage lands.

reasons: Grock's wall and airborne pressure can restrict escape routes for Odette's AoE damage.
Odette benefits when Grock creates a trapped or slowed fight area before she commits.

proof.summary: Grock creates a restricted fight area, while Odette uses that area to land repeated magic damage.
worksBestWhen: Grock controls choke points before Odette channels.
Odette waits until enemy mobility is limited.
failureCases: Grock separates enemies away from Odette's damage area.
The fight happens in open space.

reasons: Grock can absorb pressure and disrupt enemy formation before Claude enters with AoE damage.
Claude can use Grock's frontline disruption as a safer timing for Blazing Duet.

proof.summary: Grock starts or disrupts the fight, while Claude follows once enemies are displaced or distracted.
worksBestWhen: Grock forces crowd control before Claude enters.
Claude has a clear retreat or reset path.
failureCases: Claude enters while enemy control is still available.

reasons: Grock's terrain and frontline durability can protect Irithel's kiting path.
Irithel benefits when Grock controls enemy approach angles and creates space for mobile attacks.

proof.summary: Grock controls space, while Irithel uses that space to keep attacking while moving.
worksBestWhen: Grock uses walls to cut off divers.
Irithel plays around Grock's terrain control.
failureCases: Grock walls Irithel away from safety.
Irithel is behind in item timing.
